reason: The statement does not apply to this text fragment. In this passage, King Josiah is depicted as someone who follows the commands given by Yahweh through the discovery of the book of the law and his subsequent actions in purging idolatry and restoring the worship of the one true God. He humbles himself before the LORD, enquires of the LORD for guidance, and diligently carries out the instructions found in the book of the law. His actions demonstrate obedience to divine commands rather than personal initiative.
